BermudanCallProvision.description:
Bermudan call provision for callable exotics.

Allows the issuer to terminate the note on specified call dates
at a specified call price (typically par). Currently consumed by the
Callable Range Accrual note (PRDC is not implemented, and the Snowball
pricer rejects callable provisions).

# Fields

- `call_dates`: Sorted ascending dates on which the issuer may call.
- `call_price`: Fraction of notional returned at exercise (1.0 = par).
- `lockout_periods`: Number of initial coupon periods during which
  the call right cannot be exercised.

call_dates.description: Dates on which the issuer can call (must be sorted ascending).

call_price.description: Call price (fraction of notional, typically 1.0 = par).

lockout_periods.description: Lockout period in number of coupon periods before first call.

BoundsType.description: Specifies how the range bounds are interpreted.

oneOf.description:
Bounds are absolute price levels (e.g., 4500.0 for SPX).
This is the market standard for rate-linked range accruals.

Bounds are relative to initial spot (e.g., 0.95 = 95% of initial).
Common for equity-linked range accruals.

QuantoSpec.description:
Quanto adjustment parameters for instruments where payoff currency differs from
underlying currency.

correlation.description:
Correlation between the underlying asset and the FX rate.
Must be in [-1, 1].

fx_spot_id.description:
FX spot price identifier (for proper quanto vol lookup).
Falls back to ATM approximation (1.0) if not provided.

fx_vol_surface_id.description: FX volatility surface ID (required for quanto vol lookup).

RangeAccrual.description:
Range accrual instrument.

Range accrual notes pay coupons that accrue only when a reference rate or asset
stays within a specified range. The accrual is proportional to the number of
observation dates where the underlying is within [lower_bound, upper_bound].

# Bounds Interpretation

The `bounds_type` field controls how `lower_bound` and `upper_bound` are interpreted:
- `Absolute`: Bounds are absolute price levels (e.g., 4500.0 for SPX at 4700)
- `RelativeToInitialSpot`: Bounds are multipliers of the initial spot (e.g., 0.95 = 95%)

# Historical Fixings

For mid-life valuations, use `past_fixings_in_range` to specify how many past
observations were in range. The pricer will add this to expected future fixings.

accrual_start_date.description:
Contractual accrual-period start date.

When omitted, the legacy representation infers the start by stepping
one first-observation interval backward. Single-observation contracts
must provide this field explicitly.

attributes.description: Attributes for scenario selection and grouping

bounds_type.description: How to interpret the range bounds (default: Absolute)

coupon_rate.description: Coupon rate earned when in range (must be >= 0)

discount_curve_id.description: Discount curve ID for present value calculations

lower_bound.description: Lower bound of accrual range (interpretation depends on bounds_type)

observation_dates.description: Observation dates for range checking (must be sorted ascending)

past_fixings_in_range.description:
Number of past observations that were in range (for mid-life valuations).
If None, past observations are not included in the accrual calculation.

payment_date.description: Optional payment date (defaults to last observation date)

projection_curve_id.description: Projection curve for a rate-linked range accrual.

quanto.description:
Optional quanto adjustment parameters. When provided, applies a drift
correction for instruments whose payoff currency differs from the
underlying asset currency.

rate_index_id.description: Explicit rate index for rate-linked range accruals.

reference_tenor.description: Contractual tenor of the observed reference rate.

total_past_observations.description:
Total number of past observations (for mid-life valuations).
Must be provided if `past_fixings_in_range` is set.

upper_bound.description: Upper bound of accrual range (must be > lower_bound)

instrument.description: The Callable Range Accrual instrument definition
